mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack signals severe economic weakness and collapsing consumer spending, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal yields a negative information coefficient over a 12-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving aggregate demand growth that reliably produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reveals structural supply bottlenecks and weak capital investment, forcing equity valuations lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of contraction episodes, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index readings force central banks to maintain accommodative liquidity conditions in the short term, reducing real yields and generating immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 65 percent of high-inflation months, or the signal produces a statistically insignificant Sharpe ratio over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter, aggregate demand collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of low-growth quarters, or the threshold signal yields a negative cumulative alpha over a 36-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting wage growth and consumer spending to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of triggered periods, or interaction effects show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, persistent low federal funds rates signal delayed policy tightening and rising term premiums, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation low-rate periods, or the regime-conditional signal shows a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 24-month windows.
